agenticSetups.title: Alameda County Urgent Care — 5 Locations
agenticSetups.useCaseSummary: A 5-location urgent care practice in Alameda County, CA (all Pacific time). Locations: Albany, Berkeley Hills, Castro Valley, Dublin, and Emeryville. Offers a single service — Urgent Care Visit (20-minute duration, 10-minute booking cadence) — which is location-scheduled (walk-in / pre-book capacity model, no specific provider required). Weekday hours: all locations open at 8am PT; Albany and Berkeley Hills close at 11pm, the other three at midnight. Weekend hours vary by location (shorter windows, later opens). Pre-book capacity is tiered across the day: 3 concurrent slots before 4pm, 1 from 4–9pm, and 2 from 9pm to close on weekdays; 1 concurrent slot for all weekend hours. Capacity numbers are intended as tunable defaults that staff can adjust per location/day/hour as staffing and walk-in volume dictate.

content.text:
Hi! I'll help you describe your practice so we can spin up a sched-linx scenario that matches how you actually run.

Let's start with a couple of orientation questions:

1. What kind of practice is it? (e.g. specialty consult clinic, urgent care, primary care, physical therapy, group therapy)
2. Roughly how big? (Number of providers, or number of patients you typically see in a day.)

Once I have that, we'll get into the specifics — the services you offer, whether you have rooms or equipment that constrain scheduling, and the pattern of how appointments are booked (scheduled per-provider, walk-in against capacity, or a mix).
Ours is a 5 location urgent care practice in Alameda County, CA. We see roughly 300 patients a day combined across our 5 locations.

Great, an urgent care practice in Alameda County — so we're talking Pacific time across all five locations. 300 patients a day across 5 sites is roughly 60 per location, which is a solid volume.

A couple of follow-up questions:

1. **What are the names of your five locations?** (Even informal names like "San Leandro" or "Dublin" are fine.)
2. **Urgent care is typically walk-in / capacity-based rather than booked against a specific provider** — does that match how you work, or do any of your locations (or services) involve patients booking with a specific doctor or provider?

1. Albany, Berkley Hills, Castro Valley, Dublin, Emeryville
2. Most of our volume is walk-ins, but we do offer pre-booked visits as well in order to expedite the intake process when the patient reaches the office. Prebooked appointments are never with a specific provider, though. We set a limited prebook capacity which is carved out of our overall capacity - it's based on how many providers we expect to be working as well as expected walk-in volume.

That's a clean setup — all location-scheduled, no provider-specific booking. The pre-book capacity is just a slice of your overall shift capacity, and your team sets those numbers based on expected staffing and walk-in volume. That maps nicely onto our primitives.

A few more questions to flesh this out:

1. **What services do you offer?** For example: standard urgent care visit, sports physical, occupational health / worker's comp, COVID testing, laceration repair — or just one general "urgent care visit" type?
2. **What are your typical operating hours?** Same across all five locations, or do some differ?
3. **Do you have any rooms or equipment that constrain scheduling** (e.g., a procedure room for lacerations, X-ray, or a dedicated COVID testing bay)?

1. We're just standard urgent care visit right now. We do plan to add services like sports physicals and occupational health in the future, but for now we're a pure urgent care. We would not want to commit to a system that would not allow us to easily introduce additional services, but for now just urgent care. 
2. All locations open at 8am Monday-Friday. Albany and Berkley Hills close at 11pm on weekdays, while other locations are open until midnight. On weekends, Castro Valley and Dublin open at 11am and close at 7pm. Albany opens at 2pm and closes at 9pm. Berkely Hills opens at 1pm and closes at 8pm. Emeryville is open from 4pm to 10pm. 
3. Nope, all our rooms accommodate any visit. Rooms are never a factor in our capacity. Providers are always the scarce resource that determines capacity.

Good news on the future services — adding new service types later is straightforward in sched-linx, so no concerns there.

Let me make sure I have the hours right before I record anything:

**Weekdays (Mon–Fri):**
| Location | Open | Close |
|---|---|---|
| Albany | 8am | 11pm |
| Berkeley Hills | 8am | 11pm |
| Castro Valley | 8am | midnight |
| Dublin | 8am | midnight |
| Emeryville | 8am | midnight |

**Weekends (Sat–Sun):**
| Location | Open | Close |
|---|---|---|
| Albany | 2pm | 9pm |
| Berkeley Hills | 1pm | 8pm |
| Castro Valley | 11am | 7pm |
| Dublin | 11am | 7pm |
| Emeryville | 4pm | 10pm |

Does that look right? Also, one quick question: **what's the duration of a standard urgent care visit** — and do you want to set a booking cadence (i.e., how often a new pre-book slot opens up)? For example, some urgent cares do 20-minute visits but open a new slot every 15 minutes.

Yes, that is spot on! All visits are 20 minutes, but we do prefer to make booking slots available every 10 minutes for maximum flexibility. If we can only expose slots every 15 or 20 minutes though that could still work, but would not be ideal.

Good news — sched-linx supports duration and cadence as separate values, so 20-minute visits with slots opening every 10 minutes is fully expressible. No compromise needed there.

One last thing before I start recording: **what capacity numbers should I use for the schedule windows?** Specifically:

- How many concurrent patients can typically be seen at once at each location (this drives the overall shift capacity)?
- Or if it's the same across all locations, just give me that one number.

(Remember, you mentioned pre-book capacity is a carved-out slice of overall capacity — I'll set the location schedule capacity to reflect the pre-book portion specifically, since walk-ins don't consume pre-booked slots. So if you want to share that split too, great — otherwise a single "total pre-book slots per shift" number works.)

Ok, so the question is how many prebook appointments are we able to support at any given time at all of our locations? If so that number varies across the day and across locations. It's important we retain the ability to tune that number as needed for each location/day/hour.

Totally understood — and that flexibility is exactly what sched-linx is built for. You can have as many capacity windows as you need, each with its own number, so you can tune by location, day, and time of day independently. There's no requirement to use a single flat number.

Rather than me guessing at your capacity curve, let me ask: **do you want to seed this scenario with some representative numbers, or would you prefer I set up the structure (locations, service, schedules) with placeholder capacity values that your team can tune after committing?**

If you want representative numbers, even a rough example like "at Albany on a weekday morning we'd allow 3 pre-books at a time, afternoons 5, evenings 2" would be enough for me to model the pattern — then your team adjusts from there.

Ok, I see. Let's do rough defaults for now if we can. 3 for each location before 4pm on weekdays and 1 up until 9pm, and then 2 until close approximates a typical setting. If you put that for all that would be fine. Lets do a capacity of 1 for all locations, all hours on the weekends.
Perfect, that's a clear pattern. Let me record everything now — I'll do it in the right order: locations first, then the service, then all the schedule windows.
Now the service:

Now I'll record all the schedule windows across the representative week (Mon Jul 20 – Sun Jul 26, 2026). This will be a big parallel burst — weekday windows split into three capacity bands (cap 3 before 4pm, cap 1 from 4pm–9pm, cap 2 from 9pm–close), plus weekend windows at cap 1. All times converted from Pacific to UTC (PDT = UTC−7).

**Weekday time bands in UTC:**
- 8am–4pm PT = 15:00–23:00 UTC → cap 3
- 4pm–9pm PT = 23:00–04:00 UTC (next day) → cap 1
- 9pm–11pm PT (Albany/Berkeley Hills) = 04:00–06:00 UTC → cap 2
- 9pm–midnight PT (Castro Valley/Dublin/Emeryville) = 04:00–07:00 UTC → cap 2
Now the remaining 4 weekdays (Tue–Fri, Jul 21–24). I'll emit all 3 bands × 5 locations × 4 days in parallel:
Am I done? Should I click "commit scenario"?

Not quite yet — I hit a technical snag mid-way through recording the schedule windows (the batch was too large and some failed). The core structure is in place (all 5 locations, the Urgent Care Visit service, and Monday's schedule windows), but I still need to finish recording Tuesday through Friday and the weekend schedules.

Let me continue in smaller batches. Starting with Tuesday–Wednesday:
Good — Tuesday done. Now Wednesday (Jul 23 open → Thu Jul 24 close bands):
Now Thursday and Friday:
Weekdays are done. Now the weekends (Sat Jul 25 – Sun Jul 26), all cap 1:

All schedules are in! Here's a full summary of what's been recorded before you commit:

---

**Alameda County Urgent Care — 5 Locations**

**Locations (all Pacific time):** Albany, Berkeley Hills, Castro Valley, Dublin, Emeryville

**Service:** Urgent Care Visit — 20-minute duration, 10-minute booking cadence, location-scheduled (no specific provider required)

**Weekday schedules (Mon–Fri, week of Jul 20–24):**
- 8am–4pm PT → capacity **3**
- 4pm–9pm PT → capacity **1**
- 9pm–close PT → capacity **2**
  - Albany & Berkeley Hills close at 11pm; Castro Valley, Dublin & Emeryville close at midnight

**Weekend schedules (Sat–Sun, Jul 25–26):**
- Albany: 2pm–9pm PT → capacity **1**
- Berkeley Hills: 1pm–8pm PT → capacity **1**
- Castro Valley & Dublin: 11am–7pm PT → capacity **1**
- Emeryville: 4pm–10pm PT → capacity **1**

---

Does everything look right? If so, just say the word and I'll finalize the plan so you can commit!
Yes, looks right!

content.text:
You're all set! The plan is finalized and ready to commit. 🎉

A few things to keep in mind as you get started in sched-linx:
- **Capacity tuning** is easy — just edit individual schedule windows per location, day, and time band as your staffing or demand changes.
- **Adding new services** (sports physicals, occupational health, etc.) when you're ready is straightforward — you'll just add the service and layer in any new schedule windows or provider schedules needed at that time.

Go ahead and hit **Commit Scenario** whenever you're ready!
